private.name: ClearSpace
private.rationale: Same sector (Space & Aerospace), same stage (Series A), post-money $400M

data.narrative:
Epic Aerospace, a Series A-stage space and aerospace company founded by Ignacio Belieres Montero, is evaluated against a peer universe of five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies. The comparable set includes Aalyria ($1.3B, Series B), Stoke Space ($1.0B, Series B), ClearSpace ($400M, Series A), Hubble Network ($400M, Series B), and Sift ($274M, Series B).

Peer-median valuation is the applicable triangulation method, given the absence of disclosed revenue for Epic Aerospace. The five-company peer cohort yields a median valuation of $400M, with a range spanning $274M to $1.3B. This method captures variation across stage progression and company maturity within frontier-space infrastructure. Public comparables in Space & Aerospace trade at a 45.3x price-to-sales median across six tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL), but this method cannot be applied without target revenue.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $340M to $460M, centered on $400M. Key uncertainties include Epic Aerospace's revenue generation trajectory, technology maturity relative to Series B peers, and whether it commands a discount for earlier-stage positioning or a premium for differentiated capability. The peer range ($274M–$1.3B) is wide, reflecting heterogeneity in space-sector business models and execution risk. Investors should demand clarity on traction metrics and funding history (currently listed as undisclosed) to refine positioning within the range.
